title: Ranbir Singh Jolly vs Harminder Singh Hora on 3 March, 2017
body: Ranbir Singh Jolly vs Harminder Singh Hora on 3 March, 2017 WP-1250-2017 (RANBIR SINGH JOLLY Vs HARMINDER SINGH HORA) 03-03-2017 Shri P.C. Nair, learned counsel for the petitioner. Petitioner has filed the present petition being aggrieved by the orders dated 03.02.2017 and 14.02.2017. According to the petitioner, he took a property situated at 4, New Palasia, Harshdeep Plaza, Indore from the respondent on monthly rent of Rs.1,20,000/- per month. In the month of October-November'2015, he setup his showroom and the factory, but the respondent avoided to execute rent agreement and started harassing the petitioner by cutting the power supply. By his conduct, the petitioner has suffered substantial loss and because of the harassment suffered by the petitioner, he decided to wind up his business in the said premises in February'2016 and told the respondent that he shall vacate the premises and asked respondent to return the security amount of Rs.3,60,000/-. In order to remove the machineries, the petitioner was required to demolish the wall. The petitioner filed the suit for torts to claim damages and recovery of loss from the respondent. In the said suit, petitioner filed an application under Order 29 Rule 9 of CPC praying the Court to appoint the Commissioner for inspection of the suit premises and prepare the list of the machinery and items lying at the suit premises. The said application was withdrawn and another application under Section 75 read with Section 151 of CPC was filed. Vide order dated 03.02.2017, application under Section 75 read with Section 151 of CPC was dismissed on the ground that application is filed under the wrong provision. Thereafter, the petitioner filed an another application on 09.02.2017 without mentioning any provision of law. Vide order dated 14.02.2017, the said application was also dismissed, hence the present petition. In these two applications, the petitioner has simply prayed that one commission may be appointed, so that he can prepare list of the machineries and other articles lying in the rented premises so that the petitioner can remove them to avoid further dispute and he has also prayed that commission be directed to make a video recording while removing the articles. The learned Court has rejected the application on the ground that there is no provision under Section 75 of the CPC as well as under Order 26 Rule 9 of CPC for the relief claimed. Section 75 of the Code of Civil Procedure is reproduced below: â75. Power of court to issue commissions.- Subject to such conditions and limitations as may be prescribed, the court may issue a commissionâ (a) to examine any person; (b) to make a local investigation; (c) to examine or adjust accounts; or (d) to make a partition; (e) to hold a scientific, technical, or expert investigation; (f) to conduct sale of property which is subject to speedy and natural decay and which is in the custody of the court pending the determination of the suit; (g) to perform any ministerial act. â Under the aforesaid provision, the Court may issue a commission subject to such condition and limitation to make a local investigation under Sub-Clause ''B'' and to perform any ministerial Act under Sub-Clause ''G''. The petitioner has simply prayed that the commission be appointed to prepare an inventory, so that he can remove his machinery and other articles lying in the suit premises which belongs to the petitioner. Let the trial Court consider again the application filed under Section 75 read with Section 151 of CPC afresh because there is a provision to give direction under Section 75 of CPC. Even under Order 26 Rule 9 of CPC , the Court has enough power to direct for a local investigation for the purpose of deciding any dispute as it think fit. The power is very wide, therefore, this matter is remanded back to the learned civil Judge, Class-I to decide afresh the applications filed by the petitioner. The trial Court is directed to decide the application for appointment of Commissioner along with the application for temporary injunction. Let both the applications be decided within 10 days from production of certified copy of this order. However, if the respondent is aggrieved by this order, he is free to approach this Court for recalling of this order. With the aforesaid observations, petition is disposed of. (VIVEK RUSIA) JUDGE
